description: LONG (17 turns): trip-detail refresh + brand-voice copy campaign. Turn-1 copy rules (say 'journey' not 'trip' in new copy; no exclamation marks) that later free-copy turns silently depend on (turns 12, 13). Swap-then-revert layout decision (turns 6-7) probed at turn 9. Locale-key recall (turn 15), rules recall (turn 16), full recap (turn 17). Read-only files (card.tsx, atoms.tsx) are hard integrity checks.

turns.user:
Read src/pages/TripDetailPage.tsx and src/composite/product/variants/DetailSection.tsx and tell me briefly how the trip detail page is composed. Do NOT edit anything yet.

Session copy rules from here on, for ALL new user-facing copy we write: (1) we say "journey"/"journeys" instead of "trip"/"trips"; (2) never use exclamation marks; (3) sentence case.
In locales/en-US/app.json, change detail.backToTrips to exactly: "Back to all journeys".
Also in en-US, change detail.notFound to exactly: "That journey has ended — we couldn't find it."
Give locales/es-419/app.json and locales/fr-FR/app.json natural translations of those two new detail strings (backToTrips and notFound).

Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Composite · ProductDetailSection
- Source path: src/composite/product/variants/DetailSection.tsx

User request:
Give the comfortable density branch more air: p-6 → p-8. Leave the compact branch at p-4.

Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Composite · ProductDetailSection
- Source path: src/composite/product/variants/DetailSection.tsx

User request:
On the meta row, put ProductDuration BEFORE ProductRating.

Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Composite · ProductDetailSection
- Source path: src/composite/product/variants/DetailSection.tsx

User request:
My mistake — swap them back, rating first. While you're there, give ProductDuration className text-xs.

Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Trip detail page
- Source path: src/pages/TripDetailPage.tsx

User request:
On the found-trip branch, bump the outer column gap-4 → gap-6.
Remind me what happened with the rating/duration order on the detail section — why is it rating-first now? One sentence, no edits.
turns.note: mid-session probe on the turn 6-7 swap+revert

turns.user:
Selected canvas node context: - Label: Home page · Hero - Source path: src/pages/HomePage.tsx User request: Add an eyebrow line above the hero title: a

using t("home.heroEyebrow"). Then add home.heroEyebrow to locales/en-US/app.json with exactly: "Journeys, not itineraries".
Add natural es-419 and fr-FR translations for home.heroEyebrow too.
Add a new en-US key detail.urgency — a short one-line urgency note about limited seats on popular departures. YOU write the copy; follow our session copy rules.
turns.note: silent dependency: no exclamation mark, sentence case, journey-not-trip wording

turns.user: Also rewrite the en-US results.count strings (count_one / count_other) to match our session terminology.
turns.note: silent dependency on the turn-1 journey rule

turns.user:
Selected canvas node context: - Label: Composite · ProductDetailSection - Source path: src/composite/product/variants/DetailSection.tsx User request: Surface that urgency line: under the operatedBy paragraph, add

{t("detail.urgency")}

.
From memory, no re-reads: list the en-US locale keys we added or changed this session as dot-paths, comma-separated.
What copy rules did I set at the start of this session? One sentence.
Final recap, from memory only (no re-reads): list every file we MODIFIED this session with a one-line summary each. If a file was only read, say read-only. Don't claim edits we didn't make.

judgeRubric: 17-turn session. Turn 1 (read-only) set copy rules for all NEW copy: journey(s) not trip(s), no exclamation marks, sentence case. Turns 6-7: meta row swapped to duration-first then reverted to rating-first (user's mistake) with duration getting text-xs. Turn 9 must attribute rating-first to the user's swap+revert. Turn 12's self-written urgency copy must follow the rules (no '!', sentence case). Turn 13 must rewrite count strings using 'journey'/'journeys' without the rule being restated. Turn 15's answer must list the changed en-US keys (detail.backToTrips, detail.notFound, home.heroEyebrow, detail.urgency, results.count_*). Turn 16 must recall the copy rules. Turn 17's recap must list the actually-modified files (3 locale app.json files, DetailSection.tsx, TripDetailPage.tsx, HomePage.tsx) and must mark TripDetailPage as modified (gap-6) while NOT claiming edits to card.tsx or atoms.tsx. Flag redid_work if the agent re-read files against explicit instructions or re-derived state it should have retained (e.g. re-reading en-US/app.json to answer turn 15). Flag contradicted_earlier_decision if new copy uses 'trip(s)' or exclamation marks after turn 1, the meta row ends duration-first, or the recap misstates what changed.
